title: Find Equilibrium Point

content.problemStatement:
You are given a 0−indexed0-indexed0−indexed integer array numsnumsnums. Your task is to find the leftmost equilibrium index in the array. An equilibrium index is defined as an index iii such that the sum of the elements to the left of iii is equal to the sum of the elements to the right of iii.

Formally, the equilibrium index iii satisfies the condition:

sum(nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i−1])=sum(nums[i+1]+nums[i+2]+...+nums[nums.length−1])sum(nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i−1])=sum(nums[i+1]+nums[i+2]+...+nums[nums.length−1])sum(nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i−1])=sum(nums[i+1]+nums[i+2]+...+nums[nums.length−1])

**Conditions**:


If iii is 000, the left sum is considered 000.
If iii is (n−1)(n - 1)(n−1), the right sum is considered 000.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line contains an integer NNN, length of array.
The next line contain an array numsnumsnums of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, return the leftmost equilibrium index if it exists, or return −1-1−1 if there is no such index.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the leftmost equilibrium index, or -1 if no such index exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: index 0 has left sum 0 and right sum (-1+2+1-2)=0, so answer 0.
Test case 2: index 2 has left sum (1+(-1))=0 and right sum 0, so answer 2.
Test case 3: no index satisfies the condition, output -1.
With a single element, both left and right sums are 0, so index 0 is an equilibrium.
No index yields equal left and right sums; therefore output is -1.

solution.approach: Compute the total sum of the array once. Then iterate from left to right keeping a running left‑sum. For each position i, the right‑sum equals total‑left‑arr[i]. If left‑sum equals right‑sum, i is an equilibrium index. Because we stop at the first match, the answer is the leftmost equilibrium. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space besides the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra (the input array itself is required)

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());long[] a=new long[N];StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long total=0;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){a[i]=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());total+=a[i];}long left=0;int ans=-1;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){long right=total-left-a[i];if(left==right){ans=i;break;}left+=a[i];}sb.append(ans).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
